mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and real GDP indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a quarterly regression.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening supply-side momentum that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of contraction periods, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising core inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate forces tighter financial conditions that compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and steady-rate regime produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect reverses sign during periods of supply-driven inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumer spending capacity, which directly boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 4-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with payroll growth (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts below 1.1 million units signals collapsing construction demand and reduced wealth effects, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of sub-threshold months, or the threshold effect disappears when mortgage rates fall below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates supply-side bottlenecks that constrain output growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict returns when capacity utilization remains below 78%.
